steps.title: Group the four terms in pairs
Factor each pair
steps.content: From the first pair, factor $x$: $x(3x-5)$. From the second pair, factor $2$: $2(3x-5)$. The shared factor is $3x-5$, giving $(3x-5)(x+2)$.

steps.title: Match against each choice
steps.content:
Compare the factors $(3x-5)$ and $(x+2)$ to the options:
  • $x-2$: sign is wrong; the linear factor is $x+2$.
  • $x+5$: not a factor.
  • $3x-5$: appears in the factorization.
  • $3x+2$: not a factor.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
